name: elementary differential
statement_text:
Given a tree t and a function f : RN → RN , analytic in a
neighbourhood of y, the ‘elementary differential’ F (t)(y) is defined by

                 F (τ )(y) = f (y),                                                                      (310g)
                                            (m)
    F ([t1 , t2 , . . . , tm ]) = f               (y)(F (t1 )(y), F (t2 )(y), . . . , F (tm )(y)).       (310h)

Note that the tensor interpretation of (310h) is written as

           F i ([t1 , t2 , . . . , tm ]) = fji1 ,j2 ,...,jm F j1 (t1 )F j2 (t2 ) · · · F jm (tm ).

   The elementary differentials up to order 5 are shown in Table 310(II). Note
that we use the same abbreviation as in Table 310(I), in which f, f  , . . . denote
f (y(x)), f (y(x)) , . . . . The values of α(t) are also shown; their significance will
be explained in the next subsection.
   As part of the equipment we need to manipulate expressions involving
elementary differentials we consider the value of

As a formal series, this can be evaluated using the following result:

variables.name: f
variables.definition: f : R^N → R^N, analytic in a neighbourhood of y

variables.name: y
variables.definition: y(x), the solution function of the ODE

variables.name: f^{(m)}
variables.definition: m-th derivative of f, a tensor

variables.name: f_{j_1, j_2, ..., j_m}^i
variables.definition: Component notation for the m-th derivative tensor f^{(m)}

preamble: The context involves representing derivatives of the ODE solution y(x) using trees and elementary differentials. Trees t are built from vertices and edges, with specific trees corresponding to terms in derivatives of y. The notation f, f', f'' abbreviates f(y(x)), f'(y(x)), f''(y(x)).
